title: [FIRRTL] combinational loop detection
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    output a: UInt<11>
    output b: UInt<11>
    a <= b
    b <= a
```

Compiled with `firtool a_top_mod.fir --lower-to-hw --expand-whens --infer-widths --lowering-options=noAlwaysFF --verilog -o=a_top_mod_new.v` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  output [10:0] a, b);

  wire [10:0] _a_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
  wire [10:0] _b_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3

  wire [10:0] _T = _b_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:5:7
  assign _a_output = _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:5:7
  wire [10:0] _T_0 = _a_output;	// a_top_mod.fir:6:7
  assign _b_output = _T_0;	// a_top_mod.fir:6:7
  assign a = _T_0;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
  assign b = _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Error message:

```
Exception in thread "main" firrtl.transforms.CheckCombLoops$CombLoopException: : [module top_mod] Combinational loop detected:
```
